SEARCH
Package com.stevenlagoy.presidency.characters

Class PoliticalActor

java.lang.Object
com.stevenlagoy.presidency.characters.CharacterJava
com.stevenlagoy.presidency.characters.PoliticalActorJava
All Implemented Interfaces:
HasPersonality, Jsonic<Character>, Repr<Character>
Direct Known Subclasses:
Candidate, FederalOfficial, LocalOfficial, StateOfficial
public class PoliticalActor extends Character implements HasPersonality
A Character subclass which represents a Character who may interact with political systems, like leaders, lobbyists, or candidates.

Contains fields and methods which describe a PoliticalActor's alignments and positions.

See Also:
  • Character
  • HasPersonality

    Enum for Education levels, based on ISCED 9-level framework.
    static enum 
    PoliticalActor.PoliticalRole

    Creates a PoliticalActor by deep-copying the fields of the passed Character object, and fills the other fields with the passed values.

  • Constructor Details

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(CharacterManager cm, DemographicsManager dm, MapManager mm, NameManager nm)
      Creates a PoliticalActor with all fields generated by the CharacterManager.
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        Character#Character()

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(PoliticalActor other)
      Deep-copies the existing fields of another PoliticalActor object. Optionally adds to CharacterManager's list of Characters.
      Parameters:
      other - PoliticalActor to copy fields from.
      addToCharacterList - Boolean indicating whether to add the created object to the CharacterManager's list.

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(Character character)
      Creates a PoliticalActor by deep-copying the fields of the passed Character object, with all other fields generated by the CharacterManager.
      Parameters:
      character - Character to copy fields from.
      addToCharacterList - Boolean indicating whether to add the created object to the CharacterManager's list.
      See Also:
      • Character(Character)

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(String buildstring)
      Creates a PoliticalActor parsed from the Repr buildstring.
      Parameters:
      buildstring - Valid Repr-format buildstring.
      addToCharacterList - Boolean indicating whether to add the created object to the CharacterManager's list.

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(core.JSONObject json)

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(Character character, int cash, PoliticalActor.Education education, int[] alignments, List<Experience> experiences, Skills skills, List<Position> positions, Personality personality, Set<PoliticalActor.PoliticalRole> roles)
      Creates a PoliticalActor by deep-copying the fields of the passed Character object, and fills the other fields with the passed values. The CharacterManager will generate values for any null values passed.
      Parameters:
      character - Character to copy fields from.
      cash - Funds currently usable by this PoliticalActor.
      education - Highest education level attained by this PoliticalActor.
      alignments - Int array of two values between -100 and +100, which represents the PoliticalActor's Auth/Lib and Right/Left alignments.
      experiences - List of Experiences representing the experiences of this PoliticalActor.
      skills - Skills object representing the skills and aptitude of this PoliticalActor.
      positions - List of the positions on issues held by this PoliticalActor.
      personality - Personality object for this PoliticalActor's personality.
      roles - Set of Roles for this PoliticalActor's roles. If empty or null, Role.NONE will be added.

    • PoliticalActor

      public PoliticalActor(CharacterManager cm, DemographicsManager dm, MapManager mm, NameManager nm, Demographics demographics, Name name, Municipality birthplaceMunicipality, Municipality currentLocationMunicipality, Municipality residenceMunicipality, LocalDate birthday, CharacterModel appearance, int cash, PoliticalActor.Education education, int[] alignments, List<Experience> experiences, Skills skills, List<Position> positions, Personality personality, Set<PoliticalActor.PoliticalRole> roles)
      Creates a PoliticalActor, filling fields with the passed values. The CharacterManager will generate values for any null values passed.
      Parameters:
      demographics - Demographics object representing this Character's demographic memberships.
      name - Name object representing this Character's name.
      birthplaceMunicipality - Municipality in which this Character was born.
      currentLocationMunicipality - Municipality to which this Character is currently nearest.
      residenceMunicipality - Municipality in which this Character currently resides (primary residence).
      birthday - Date on which this character was born.
      appearance - CharacterModel object to be rendered for this Character.
      cash - Funds currently usable by this PoliticalActor.
      education - Highest education level attained by this PoliticalActor.
      alignments - Int array of two values between -100 and +100, which represents the PoliticalActor's Auth/Lib and Right/Left alignments.
      experiences - List of Experiences representing the experiences of this PoliticalActor.
      skills - Skills object representing the skills and aptitude of this PoliticalActor.
      positions - List of the positions on issues held by this PoliticalActor.
      personality - Personality object for this PoliticalActor's personality.
      roles - Set of roles for this PoliticalActor's roles. If empty or null, Role.NONE will be added.
      See Also:
      • invalid reference 
        Character#Character(Demographics, Name, Municipality, Municipality, Municipality, Date, CharacterModel)
      • invalid reference 
        #PoliticalActor(Character, int, Education, int[], List, Skills, List, Personality, Role)

    • evaluateAction

      public float evaluateAction()
      Description copied from interface: HasPersonality
      Evaluate the given action in alignment with this Character's personality.
      Specified by:
      evaluateAction in interface HasPersonality
      Returns:
      A float between +1 and -1 which represents the liklihood of this Character to take the given action.
